Change the temporal resolution of a MiltSeries

Source: R/series-manipulate.R
milt_resample.Rd

Aggregates the series to a lower frequency by applying agg_fn within each period bucket.

Usage

milt_resample(series, period, agg_fn = mean)

Arguments

series

A MiltSeries object.

period

Target period as a string: "daily", "weekly", "monthly", "quarterly", or "annual".

agg_fn

Aggregation function. Default mean. Must accept a numeric vector and return a scalar (e.g., sum, median, max).

Value

A MiltSeries at the new frequency.

See also

Other series: milt_add_covariates(), milt_concat(), milt_diagnose(), milt_fill_gaps(), milt_get_covariates(), milt_head(), milt_plot_acf(), milt_plot_decomp(), milt_series(), milt_split(), milt_split_at(), milt_tail(), milt_window(), plot.MiltSeries()

Examples

# \donttest{
s <- milt_series(AirPassengers)
# Already monthly — upsample not supported, but annual works
milt_resample(s, "annual", sum)
#> # A MiltSeries: 12 x 1 [annual]
#> # Time range : 1949 — 1960
#> # Components : value
#> # Gaps       : none
#> # A tibble: 6 × 2
#>   time       value
#>   <date>     <dbl>
#> 1 1949-01-01  1520
#> 2 1950-01-01  1676
#> 3 1951-01-01  2042
#> 4 1952-01-01  2364
#> 5 1953-01-01  2700
#> 6 1954-01-01  2867
#> # … with 6 more rows
# }
